pr.title: Add deterministic dev test game states and reset coverage

comments.body: This static import pulls the entire `src/testing/testGameStates` graph into the main app bundle even though the feature is meant to be dev-only. I verified it by running `npm run build` and checking `dist/assets/index-*.js`, which still contains `priority-reset-demo`, `Talrand, Sky Summoner`, and the full test-state builder code in the production artifact. Please move the preset loader behind a DEV-only dynamic import or isolate it in a module that Vite can fully dead-code-eliminate, so testing fixtures do not ship in production.
comments.author: ironsha-bot

replies.body: Removed the static `src/testing/testGameStates` import from `src/ui/hooks/useGameEngine.ts` and moved preset loading behind a DEV-only dynamic import inside `resolveEngine()`, so Vite can eliminate the testing fixture graph from production builds. Because initialization is now async, I also added an init sequence guard so stale async loads cannot reattach an old engine after reset or unmount.
replies.author: ironsha-bot

comments.body: The PR description's `Visual evidence` section is still pointing at repo-local `artifacts/...` paths, and there are no staged media files under `.ironsha/pr-media/`. For this React/web UI change, the screenshots and Playwright video need to be copied under `.ironsha/pr-media/` and the PR description updated to link to those staged `.ironsha/pr-media/...` paths so the publish step can upload them.
comments.author: ironsha-bot

replies.body: Copied the existing Playwright screenshots and video into `.ironsha/pr-media/` and updated the PR description metadata so the `Visual evidence` section points at the staged `.ironsha/pr-media/...` paths instead of repo-local `artifacts/...` paths.
replies.author: ironsha-bot

comments.body: The test plan's proof for the normal-load and invalid-id fallback cases is not reliable enough to validate the feature. Step 2 says to verify the normal game loads "without the preset markers shown in `priority-reset-demo`", but both `.ironsha/pr-media/normal-load.png` and a live Playwright check of `/?test-game-state=does-not-exist` still show `Talrand, Sky Summoner`, which is one of the listed preset markers. Please update the PR description so the fallback/normal assertions use identifiers that are actually unique to the preset state (for example the full combination of turn/phase/life totals plus the authored board markers, and for the invalid-id case include evidence of the expected console warning in the Playwright-driven flow).
comments.author: ironsha-bot

replies.body: Updated the stored PR description to replace the weak normal/fallback assertions with concrete baseline identifiers (`Turn 1` / `Main 1`, four players at 40 life, library 67, default commander row, and absence of preset-only altered totals/support permanents). Replaced `.ironsha/pr-media/normal-load.png` and `.ironsha/pr-media/invalid-test-game-state-fallback.png` with fresh Playwright captures, and added `.ironsha/pr-media/invalid-test-game-state-console-warning.txt` plus a warning overlay in the fallback screenshot so the expected unknown-id console warning is explicitly evidenced.
replies.author: ironsha-bot

comments.body: The `Visual evidence` section is still overstating one of the staged artifacts. `.ironsha/pr-media/priority-reset-demo-board-initial.png` is not a board-only capture: it is a byte-for-byte duplicate of `.ironsha/pr-media/priority-reset-demo-initial.png`, and both files are full-page `1440x1100` screenshots. Please replace it with an actual Playwright board-only screenshot (for example from `page.locator('.arena-board').screenshot(...)`) or update the PR description to remove/relabel that evidence so the staged media accurately matches what reviewers are being asked to validate.
comments.author: ironsha-bot

replies.body: Replaced `.ironsha/pr-media/priority-reset-demo-board-initial.png` with a fresh Playwright `.arena-board` locator capture for `priority-reset-demo`, and updated the stored PR description to relabel that evidence so it accurately matches the staged artifact.
replies.author: ironsha-bot

comments.body: The PR description now points at `.ironsha/pr-media/...`, but those media files are still ignored by git (`.gitignore` line 28 matches `.ironsha/`). In this worktree `git ls-files .ironsha/pr-media` returns nothing, so the referenced screenshots/video are not actually staged for the publish step. Please unignore the PR media path as needed and add the referenced Playwright artifacts under `.ironsha/pr-media/` so reviewers and the CLI can access the exact files linked in `Visual evidence`.
comments.author: ironsha-bot

replies.body: Updated `.gitignore` to keep `.ironsha/pr-media/**` and PR metadata JSON files trackable, then added the current PR description file and every referenced Playwright artifact under `.ironsha/pr-media/` so the publish step can access the exact visual-evidence files linked in the PR description.
replies.author: ironsha-bot

comments.body: Blocking: the PR still adds review media outside the allowed staged publish path. In addition to the `.ironsha/pr-media/...` files referenced in `Visual evidence`, this diff also adds duplicate screenshots/videos under `artifacts/normal-load.png`, `artifacts/invalid-test-game-state-fallback.png`, `artifacts/priority-reset-demo-initial.png`, `artifacts/priority-reset-demo-board-initial.png`, `artifacts/priority-reset-demo-after-reset.png`, and `artifacts/videos/c72b9db8855eed9b9ff7cb1ac9f67123.webm`. The QA requirements for this flow are that the publishable evidence live under `.ironsha/pr-media/`; please remove the repo-local `artifacts/` media from the PR so there is a single authoritative staged media set for reviewers and the CLI publish step.
comments.author: ironsha-bot

replies.body: Removed the duplicate tracked screenshots and video under `artifacts/` so the PR now has a single authoritative review-media set under `.ironsha/pr-media/`, which is the only location referenced by `Visual evidence`.
replies.author: openai

replies.body: Removed the duplicate tracked review media under `artifacts/` so the PR now has a single authoritative staged evidence set under `.ironsha/pr-media/`, and updated the stored PR thread metadata with the resolution reply.

description:
**Summary**
- Add a dev-only `test-game-state` query param that can boot the app from a checked-in preset, fall back to the normal game for unknown ids, and preserve the selected preset when `New Game` is used.
- Introduce a reusable `TestGameStateBuilder` plus a durable `priority-reset-demo` preset to create deterministic board states for Playwright and manual UI verification.
- Update engine initialization to accept a supplied initial state and clean up hook subscriptions/reset behavior so preset reloads are stable; include Playwright guidance and captured artifacts for the new flow.

**Test plan**
1. Start the app from the repo root with `npm run dev -- --host 127.0.0.1 --port 0` and note the printed `127.0.0.1:<PORT>` URL.
2. Open `http://127.0.0.1:<PORT>/` and verify the baseline normal game loads at `Turn 1` / `Main 1` with `Pass Priority` visible, all four players at `40` life, each library showing `67`, and only the default commander row (`Ayara, First of Locthwain`, `Krenko, Mob Boss`, `Talrand, Sky Summoner`, `Heliod, Sun-Crowned`) instead of the preset-only altered totals, poison counter, and support permanents from `priority-reset-demo`.
3. Open `http://127.0.0.1:<PORT>/?test-game-state=priority-reset-demo` and verify `Pass Priority` is visible, the current phase step is `Main 1`, player totals are `18` / `24` / `27` / `32`, the black seat shows `Poison 1/10`, and the board shows `Talrand, Sky Summoner`, `Rhystic Study`, `Blood Artist`, `Swiftfoot Boots`, `Command Tower`, and `Sol Ring`.
4. Click `Pass Priority` and verify the current phase step advances to `Main 2`.
5. Open settings, click `New Game`, and verify the board reloads the same preset deterministically: `Pass Priority` is visible again, the current phase step returns to `Main 1`, and the same distinctive cards and altered totals are restored.
6. Open `http://127.0.0.1:<PORT>/?test-game-state=does-not-exist` and verify the app falls back to the same baseline normal-game state from step 2 while the browser console emits `Unknown test-game-state "does-not-exist". Available ids: priority-reset-demo.`.

**Visual evidence**
- `.ironsha/pr-media/priority-reset-demo-flow.webm` — video — Playwright capture of the interactive `priority-reset-demo` flow, including initial `Main 1`, advancing priority, and using `New Game` to reload the preset deterministically.
- `.ironsha/pr-media/priority-reset-demo-initial.png` — screenshot — Playwright full-page capture of the initial `priority-reset-demo` screen in dev with the preset board loaded at `Main 1`, the altered `18` / `24` / `27` / `32` life totals, and `Pass Priority` visible.
- `.ironsha/pr-media/priority-reset-demo-board-initial.png` — screenshot — Playwright `.arena-board` locator capture of the initial `priority-reset-demo` battlefield showing the authored visible markers.
- `.ironsha/pr-media/priority-reset-demo-after-reset.png` — screenshot — Playwright full-page capture after using `New Game` while `test-game-state=priority-reset-demo` is present, showing the preset restored to its initial state.
- `.ironsha/pr-media/normal-load.png` — screenshot — Playwright full-page capture of the baseline dev load without a `test-game-state` query param, showing `Turn 1` / `Main 1`, all four players at `40` life, and the default commander row instead of the preset-only altered totals/support cards.
- `.ironsha/pr-media/invalid-test-game-state-fallback.png` — screenshot — Playwright full-page capture of the unknown-id fallback showing the same baseline normal-game state, with an added overlay of the browser console warning captured during the Playwright run.
- `.ironsha/pr-media/invalid-test-game-state-console-warning.txt` — log — Playwright-captured browser warning text from the invalid-id fallback flow, saved alongside the screenshots for reviewer inspection.
